access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q35.explanation: Câu 35 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn 1. Ý chính trong bài là: "Wars over access to water are a rising possibility and the main conflicts in Africa during the next 25 years could be over this most precious of commodities. “Potential water wars are likely in areas where rivers and lakes are shared by more than one country,” says Mark Evans, a UN worker. Evans predicts: “Population growth and economic development will lead to nearly one in two people in Africa living in countries which face water scarcity or ‘water stress’ within 25 years.” Water scarcity is defined as less than 1,000 cubic metres of water available per person per year, while water stress means less than 1,500 cubic metres of water per person per year.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q35.evidence: Wars over access to water are a rising possibility and the main conflicts in Africa during the next 25 years could be over this most precious of commodities. “Potential water wars are likely in areas where rivers and lakes are shared by more than one country,” says Mark Evans, a UN worker. Evans predicts: “Population growth and economic development will lead to nearly one in two people in Africa living in countries which face water scarcity or ‘water stress’ within 25 years.” Water scarcity is defined as less than 1,000 cubic metres of water available per person per year, while water stress means less than 1,500 cubic metres of water per person per year._

q36.explanation: Câu 36 mâu thuẫn với thông tin ở đoạn 1. Bài nêu: "Evans predicts: “Population growth and economic development will lead to nearly one in two people in Africa living in countries which face water scarcity or ‘water stress’ within 25 years.” Water scarcity is defined as less than 1,000 cubic metres of water available per person per year, while water stress means less than 1,500 cubic metres of water per person per year. The UN report says that by 2025, 12 more African countries will join the 13 that already suffer from water stress or water scarcity. What makes the water issue even more urgent is that demand for water will grow increasingly fast as larger areas are used to grow crops.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q36.evidence: Evans predicts: “Population growth and economic development will lead to nearly one in two people in Africa living in countries which face water scarcity or ‘water stress’ within 25 years.” Water scarcity is defined as less than 1,000 cubic metres of water available per person per year, while water stress means less than 1,500 cubic metres of water per person per year. The UN report says that by 2025, 12 more African countries will join the 13 that already suffer from water stress or water scarcity. What makes the water issue even more urgent is that demand for water will grow increasingly fast as larger areas are used to grow crops._

q37.explanation: Câu 37 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn 2, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "The question of how to deal with water shortages is in the forefront of the battle between environmental activists on the one hand and governments and construction firms on the other. At a recent World Summit on Sustainable Development in Johannesburg, activists mounted a campaign to halt dam construction on environmental grounds.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q37.evidence: The question of how to deal with water shortages is in the forefront of the battle between environmental activists on the one hand and governments and construction firms on the other. At a recent World Summit on Sustainable Development in Johannesburg, activists mounted a campaign to halt dam construction on environmental grounds._

q38.explanation: Câu 38 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn 3. Ý chính trong bài là: "One of the UN’s eight millennium development goals is to halve the number of people without sustainable access to safe drinking water by 2020. This had already been agreed upon at the summit, but one of the unresolved issues in the implementation plan was whether the goal on water would be extended to cover sanitation. The risks posed by water-borne diseases in the absence of sanitation facilities mean that the two goals are closely related.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q38.evidence: One of the UN’s eight millennium development goals is to halve the number of people without sustainable access to safe drinking water by 2020. This had already been agreed upon at the summit, but one of the unresolved issues in the implementation plan was whether the goal on water would be extended to cover sanitation. The risks posed by water-borne diseases in the absence of sanitation facilities mean that the two goals are closely related._

q39.explanation: Câu 39 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn 4. Ý chính trong bài là: "A number of projects and funding initiatives were also unveiled at the summit. However, implementation is always harder, as South Africa has experienced in its water programme. Graham Bennetts, a water official in the South African government explains: “Since the 1994 elections, the government has provided easy access to water for 7 million people, but extending this to a further 7 million and ensuring this progress is sustainable is one of South Africa’s foremost implementation challenges.” In South Africa, access to water is defined as 25 litres a person daily that can be accessed from within a distance of 200 metres from where they live. “Although South Africa’s feat far exceeds the UN millennium goal on water supply, severe constraints on local government capacity make a more rapid expansion difficult,” says Bennetts.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q39.evidence: A number of projects and funding initiatives were also unveiled at the summit. However, implementation is always harder, as South Africa has experienced in its water programme. Graham Bennetts, a water official in the South African government explains: “Since the 1994 elections, the government has provided easy access to water for 7 million people, but extending this to a further 7 million and ensuring this progress is sustainable is one of South Africa’s foremost implementation challenges.” In South Africa, access to water is defined as 25 litres a person daily that can be accessed from within a distance of 200 metres from where they live. “Although South Africa’s feat far exceeds the UN millennium goal on water supply, severe constraints on local government capacity make a more rapid expansion difficult,” says Bennetts._
